prompt: Maintenance on a hydraulic press requires:

options.text: Opening relief valve while hands in die
Isolation of energy, blocking/supporting ram, bleed pressure, and controlled reinstatement
Trusting pressure gauge from 1998 without check
Removing guards to see leaks better during operation

explanation: Stored energy in hydraulics can cause sudden movement; isolation and blocking are critical.

distractorExplanations.a: Hands in die during pressure release is lethal.
distractorExplanations.c: Gauge verification is part of safe isolation.
distractorExplanations.d: Guards should remain; isolation before access.

prompt: Class A fire extinguishing media include:

options.text: CO2 as primary choice on deep seated smouldering paper stock
Water on energised electrical cabinet
Water or foam suitable for ordinary combustible solids
Metal powder on wood pallets only

explanation: Class A materials (wood, paper, textiles) are fought with water/foam; deep-seated fires need prolonged application.

distractorExplanations.a: CO2 may not penetrate deep Class A smoulder.
distractorExplanations.b: CO2 may not penetrate deep Class A smoulder.
distractorExplanations.d: Metal powder is for Class D metals.

prompt: A tower crane overload indicator triggers. The operator should:

options.text: Bypass indicator to meet schedule
Swing load faster to reduce moment
Increase radius to test limit
Stop the lift, reassess load weight and rigging, and not override safety device

explanation: Safety devices on lifting equipment must not be overridden; reassess planning and load.

prompt: Electricity at work regulations emphasise:

options.text: Systems constructed, maintained, and used to prevent danger, with competence and isolation for work
Working live as default for efficiency
DIY wiring by untrained staff to save cost
Ignoring corrosion on outdoor feeds

explanation: Prevent danger through design, maintenance, competence, and safe isolation — live work only when justified.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.c: Untrained electrical work is unlawful and unsafe.
distractorExplanations.d: Corrosion can cause failure and shock.

prompt: Fixed guards on a conveyor return roller are preferable when:

options.text: Operators need daily hand contact with roller
Access is infrequent and guard removal for maintenance can be planned with isolation
Guard removal takes longer than 2 seconds occasionally
Belt speed is zero during cleaning without lock-off

explanation: Fixed guards suit infrequent access; frequent access may need interlocked guards with isolation procedures.

distractorExplanations.a: Hand contact with nip points must be prevented.
distractorExplanations.c: Occasional removal may still suit interlocked systems.
distractorExplanations.d: Zero speed without lock-off is unreliable.

prompt: Fire detection and alarm system weekly test should:

options.text: Disable system until annual contractor visit only
Test only the fire panel LCD brightness
Trigger a call point or detector zone and confirm alarm audibility at representative locations
Silence alarms permanently to reduce noise complaints

explanation: Weekly tests verify basic function; full servicing is periodic by competent person.

distractorExplanations.a: Weekly user tests complement professional service.
distractorExplanations.b: LCD test does not confirm audibility.

prompt: PAT failure on a kettle lead with exposed copper requires:

options.text: Tape over damage and return immediately
Use only if user accepts shock risk
Move to wet outdoor area without RCD
Remove from service, repair or replace, and retest before return

explanation: Failed appliances must not be used until made safe and verified.

distractorExplanations.a: Tape is inadequate repair for conductors.

prompt: Lifting equipment LOLER thorough examination timing is driven by:

options.text: Statutory intervals, examination scheme, and events like damage or overload
Paint colour change of the hook
Operator birthday
Random social media poll

explanation: LOLER requires thorough examination by competent person per schedule and after exceptional circumstances.

distractorExplanations.b: Paint change is not a LOLER trigger.

prompt: Combustible dust in a flour mill can cause:

options.text: Only slip hazards without fire risk
Explosive atmospheres and deflagration if ignited with adequate concentration and confinement
Improved LEV without ignition control
Automatic Class A rating only

explanation: Dust explosions need fuel-air mix, ignition, and confinement — process fire safety and housekeeping critical.

distractorExplanations.a: Dust also creates STF but explosion is major risk.
distractorExplanations.c: LEV helps but ignition sources must be controlled.
distractorExplanations.d: Dust explosions are not Class A water scenarios.

prompt: Safe use of an isolator for maintenance means:

options.text: Leave isolator accessible to anyone to toggle
Isolate upstream only while working live on load
Lock in off position, prove dead on load side, and communicate with all affected workers
Remove warning tags to tidy panel

explanation: Lock-out/tag-out prevents re-energisation; proving dead confirms isolation effectiveness.

distractorExplanations.b: Working live on load side with upstream isolation only is unsafe if multiple feeds exist.

prompt: PUWER requires controls to be:

options.text: Hidden behind guards during normal operation without interlocks
Identical unlabeled for all machines
Operable only by standing in nip point
Clearly marked, positioned for safe operation, and not cause additional hazard

explanation: Control design and marking support safe operation and emergency response.

distractorExplanations.a: Hidden controls impede emergency stop unless interlocked design allows.

prompt: Foam extinguisher on a solvent pool fire works by:

options.text: Separating fuel surface from oxygen with foam blanket and cooling where applicable
Increasing oxygen concentration at surface
Conducting electricity into the pool
Exploding to disperse solvent safely without training

explanation: Foam smothers flammable liquid fires; choose type suitable for polar solvents where needed.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.c: Conductivity depends on type; wrong foam on electrics is unsafe.
distractorExplanations.d: Extinguishers require trained selection and use.

prompt: Pre-use check of a chainsaw under PUWER includes:

options.text: Removing chain brake to reduce weight
Chain brake function, sharpness, guards, fuel system integrity, and PPE availability
Disabling dead-man switch for comfort
Using without eye/leg protection if experienced

explanation: Chainsaws need functional safety devices and PPE; experience does not remove need for controls.

distractorExplanations.c: Dead-man switch is essential safety feature.
distractorExplanations.d: PPE is required regardless of experience.

prompt: Electrical portable tools on construction sites should:

options.text: Use damaged casings if tape applied
Bypass RCD to stop nuisance tripping always
Be 110V centre-tapped to earth where practicable, RCD protected, and visually checked
Share one extension through water puddles

explanation: Reduced voltage and RCD use lower shock risk on harsh sites; conditions demand robust inspection.

distractorExplanations.b: Nuisance trips indicate fault investigation, not bypass.
distractorExplanations.d: Water increases conductance and shock risk.

prompt: Fire compartmentation in a building aims to:

options.text: Increase smoke spread for early detection only
Remove all fire doors for open plan aesthetics
Replace evacuation planning entirely
Limit fire and smoke spread, protecting escape routes and property

explanation: Compartment walls/doors hold fire for designed periods, supporting life safety strategy.

distractorExplanations.a: Smoke spread endangers occupants; detection complements compartmentation.
distractorExplanations.b: Fire doors are part of compartmentation.
distractorExplanations.c: Fire doors are part of compartmentation.

prompt: A nip point between meshing gears requires:

options.text: Fixed or interlocked guard preventing access to the mesh zone
Warning sticker only while gears run unguarded
Loose clothing encouraged for comfort
Frequent hand lubrication while running

explanation: Nip points cause entanglement; guarding is primary PUWER control.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.d: Hand lubrication while running risks amputation.

prompt: After maintenance, reinstatement of machine guarding should occur:

options.text: After a week's trial without guards
Before power is restored and before operators resume production
Only if an auditor visits
Never if guards slow output

explanation: Guards must be refitted and checked before normal operation resumes.

distractorExplanations.a: Trial without guards accepts unmanaged risk.
distractorExplanations.c: Auditor presence is not the trigger.
distractorExplanations.d: Production speed does not waive guarding.

prompt: Static electricity ignition in a solvent filling area is controlled by:

options.text: Wearing insulating soles only
Increasing flow velocity without grounding
Bonding and earthing, conductive footwear/floors, humidity control, and exclusion of ignition sources
Using plastic containers without earthing

explanation: Bonding/earthing prevents static buildup discharges in flammable atmospheres.

distractorExplanations.a: Insulating soles can allow charge accumulation.
distractorExplanations.d: Plastic containers need earthing/bonding in flammable filling.

prompt: LOLER lifting plan for a complex lift should address:

options.text: Only the operator's lunch preference
Colour of hi-vis vests exclusively
Social media posting schedule
Load weight, centre of gravity, crane capacity, wind, rigging, exclusion zone, and communication

explanation: Complex lifts need documented planning beyond routine simple lifts.

distractorExplanations.a: Lunch is irrelevant to lift engineering.
distractorExplanations.b: Lunch is irrelevant to lift engineering.
distractorExplanations.c: Hi-vis is PPE but plan needs engineering factors.

prompt: User weekly fire alarm test responsibility often lies with:

options.text: Employer-appointed competent person or fire warden following procedure
Any visitor to the building without training
Only the fire service during real fires
Insurance broker annually without user tests

explanation: Routine tests are employer duty; fire service responds to real incidents.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.c: Fire service does not replace weekly user tests.
distractorExplanations.d: Insurance does not conduct weekly tests.

prompt: Double insulation symbol on a Class II drill means:

options.text: Must be used only underwater
No reliance on earth wire; basic insulation plus supplementary insulation or equivalent
Requires daily PAT by law every use
Eliminates all shock risk in all conditions

explanation: Class II tools lack earth pin; design prevents accessible parts becoming live — still avoid damage and wet misuse.

distractorExplanations.c: PAT frequency is risk-based, not necessarily every use.
distractorExplanations.d: Damage or misuse can still create danger.

prompt: Emergency stop devices on machinery must be:

options.text: Hidden to prevent misuse
Slower to activate than production start
Easily accessible, clearly identifiable, and override start functions until reset
Disabled during maintenance without isolation

explanation: E-stops provide quick cessation of dangerous motion; reset should not automatically restart hazardous movement.

distractorExplanations.b: Must not be slower than start — quick access required.
distractorExplanations.d: Maintenance uses isolation; e-stop not substitute.

prompt: Fire evacuation for mobility-impaired occupants may require:

options.text: Abandoning all upper floors without assistance planning
Using lifts as first option during all fires
No communication from refuge points
Personal emergency evacuation plans (PEEPs) and refuge areas with communication

explanation: PEEPs tailor assistance, refuge, and communication for those who cannot evacuate quickly unaided.

distractorExplanations.a: Assistance planning is required, not abandonment.
distractorExplanations.b: Lifts are generally not used in fire unless specially designed evacuation lifts.
distractorExplanations.c: Lifts are generally not used in fire unless specially designed evacuation lifts.

prompt: Risk assessment for woodworking circular saw should cover:

options.text: Blade guard, riving knife, push stick, dust extraction, training, and maintenance
Only the operator's shoe size
Canteen opening hours exclusively
Whether wood is sustainably sourced only

explanation: Saw assessments address contact, kickback, dust, and noise — PUWER and COSHH interfaces.

distractorExplanations.d: Sustainability is environmental, not immediate guarding focus.

prompt: Integrating electrical safety with maintenance planning means:

options.text: Running production alongside unguarded maintenance for efficiency
Scheduling isolation windows, competent persons, spares for guards, and testing before return to service
Skipping dead test to save 30 seconds
Using unqualified staff to reset RCD repeatedly without fault finding

explanation: Planned maintenance embeds isolation, competence, and verification — aligning PUWER and electricity regs.

distractorExplanations.d: Repeated RCD trips indicate faults needing investigation.
